title: herniaria incana subsp. alpina
intro: Herniaria incana subsp. alpina, also known as alpine rumpynose or alpine herniaria, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Herniaria incana subsp. alpina include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a useful treatment for various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Herniaria incana subsp. alpina is often used in infusions, tinctures, and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
